mechanism_narrative: A sustained year-over-year increase in industrial production signals expanding supply capacity and rising output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return after a 1% increase in INDPRO YoY is negative in more than 50% of cases over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5%, it indicates a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, depressing valuation multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market that pressures wages and margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS up and UNRATE down over a month is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing cycle upturn (housing starts rising from a trough), continued increases in housing starts signal construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: housing cycle upturn (HOUST climbing from trough)
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds its 12-month average but the economy is not in an expansion, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales (RSAFS) increase by more than 1% month-over-month, it indicates robust consumer spending that drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a RSAFS MoM increase >1%, the median 20-day SPX return is not positive across all observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous year-over-year growth in industrial production and retail sales signals a synchronized economic expansion, reinforcing each other to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS are growing YoY, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising interest rate environment (federal funds rate above 2%), further increases in the fed funds rate compound monetary tightening and disproportionately harm high-valuation equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS surpasses 2%, positive monthly changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that puts upward pressure on wages, compressing corporate profit margins and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.1% month-over-month, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
